name: Regents set to vote on new Boring Company tunnel station at UNLV
summary: Months after shelving the idea over parking concerns, UNLV is back on track to host a Vegas Loop station, with the Board of Regents set to decide next week whether to approve a deal with the Boring Company.

name: U.S. News & World ranks UNLV strongly in multiple categories
summary: UNLV appears several times on U.S. News & World’s Report’s new Best Global Universities rankings.

name: TikTok Shoppers Thought They Were Bidding on iPhones. Instead, They Won Teddy Bears
summary: Sellers on TikTok used pricey items to lure bidders to “Surprise Sets” livestreams, but most of the auctions yielded only cheap prizes. Gambling experts are concerned about the potential for harm.

name: Volunteers dig into meaningful work on Catalina
summary: UNLV students help remove ice plant at White’s Landing.

name: After raising $62M, New Haven biotech Allyx Therapeutics advances experimental Alzheimer’s drug
summary: More than seven years of development, over $60 million in funding and a series of early clinical studies have led to this: Allyx Therapeutics is preparing for what may be the most consequential test in the New Haven-based biotech startup’s history.

name: Recent valley homicides highlight risks of escalating personal disputes
summary: More than three dozen homicides have been reported across the Las Vegas Valley so far this year, with many stemming from domestic disputes, verbal altercations, and ongoing interpersonal conflicts. While each case has its own circumstance, behavioral health experts and UNLV Associate Psychology Professor Dr. Stephen Benning are weighing in.

name: Woman with Advanced Alzheimer’s Disease Regains Speech, Mobility After Taking Magic Mushrooms: Report
summary: A woman with Alzheimer's disease experienced significant improvements after taking magic mushrooms, according to a new case study.

name: Nearly two-thirds of Nevada families struggle to pay high childcare costs
summary: According to the First Five Years Fund, a national early childhood advocacy organization, 28% of working families with young children in the U.S. cannot reasonably access the childcare they want or need.

name: That rattling was really a reminder: Las Vegas sits in an earthquake zone
summary: UNLV Professor Emeritus of Geology Steve Rowland recently authored a scientific paper about an intriguing, earthquake-caused geological structure — known as a clastic pipe — which was identified by avid hiker and geology enthusiast Jeffrey Cuneo near Lake Mead. The paper was published in May in the scientific journal Geology of the Intermountain West.
